title: Add login page with credentials to Draft 5

requirements.text: Implement a login page (frontend/src/components/Login.tsx) that displays a username and password form. On submission, validate the credentials using the existing useAuth hook (frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts) which checks hardcoded credentials stored in localStorage. On success, redirect the user to the Dashboard; on failure, show an inline error alert.

quotes: add the log in page with credentials to the draft 5 version without changing the rest things

acceptanceCriteria: Login page is accessible at the /login route
Username and password fields are displayed and functional
Submitting valid credentials redirects to the Dashboard
Submitting invalid credentials shows an inline error message
The login page matches the existing visual theme of the application

requirements.text: Configure the auth guard in frontend/src/App.tsx so that unauthenticated users are redirected to the login page when attempting to access protected routes (Dashboard, Inventory, Billing, Reports). Authenticated users visiting /login should be redirected to the Dashboard. All existing routes and their behavior must remain unchanged.

quotes: add the log in page with credentials to the draft 5 version without changing the rest things

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users accessing /dashboard, /inventory, /billing, or /reports are redirected to /login
Authenticated users accessing /login are redirected to /dashboard
Session state is read from localStorage on every render via the AuthGuard component
All other routes and page content remain visually and functionally identical to Draft 5

constraints: Do not change any page, component, or backend logic beyond what is needed for the login flow
Do not modify immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Preserve the Draft 5 version's visual theme and all existing functionality
nonGoals: Adding Internet Identity or any third-party authentication
Changing the backend schema or Motoko actor
Modifying the Dashboard, Inventory, Billing, or Reports pages
Implementing user registration or password reset flows
